metadata.dataset_description: Hourly sensor/meteorological time series (2016-07-01 to 2018-06-26) with seven numeric features (HUFL, HULL, MUFL, MULL, LUFL, LULL) and a numeric target OT. Likely temperature or related measurements recorded hourly from sensors or model layers.
known_data_quality_issues: Outliers detected in multiple feature columns (HUFL, HULL, MUFL, MULL, LUFL, LULL) based on z-score > 3
No missing values present (all columns fully populated)
Strong periodicity/seasonality (high autocorrelation at 24h, 168h, and ~8766h) — daily, weekly and annual cycles
Slight long-term trend (small negative linear slope in OT over the 2-year window)
Different ranges/scales across features (means and stds differ substantially)
Some features contain negative values (e.g., LULL, LUFL) — check physical validity before transforms like log

preprocessing_steps.reason: Several features show z-score outliers (>3). Remove or cap these early to avoid biasing scaling and models.
A small but measurable long-term trend exists in OT (and some features); detrending helps many forecasting models and stabilizes stationarity.
MUFL shows strong right skew (skewness > 2). Apply log transform to reduce skew and stabilize variance (only on strictly positive columns).
Features are on different scales and ranges; standardization (z-score) centers and scales inputs for most models (esp. ML and DL).
Short-term measurement noise is present; a small rolling window (3-hour) reduces high-frequency noise while preserving diurnal cycles. Use as optional preprocessing / for feature engineering.

metadata.licenses_or_usage_rights: Not provided with the file. Verify dataset license and usage rights with the data provider before distribution or commercial use.
metadata.security_or_privacy_notes: No personal identifiable information detected. Data appears to be sensor/meteorological measurements; if location or device IDs are associated elsewhere, ensure compliance with location privacy and organizational policies.

model_reasoning.LightGBM: Gradient-boosted tree model that handles large tabular datasets, robust to outliers after preprocessing, works well with engineered lag/rolling features and exogenous variables.
model_reasoning.Prophet: Designed for time series with multiple seasonalities (daily, weekly, yearly) and trend components; fast and interpretable for baseline forecasting.
model_reasoning.N-BEATS: Modern deep-learning architecture for univariate/multivariate forecasting that captures complex seasonality and trend without heavy feature engineering—good for large datasets and multi-horizon forecasts.
model_reasoning.LSTM: Sequence model (RNN) that can learn temporal dependencies and interactions across multiple sensor features; useful when you want end-to-end sequence forecasting with exogenous inputs.
